jobs.name: meta-agent-sweep
jobs.prompt:
Run the meta-agent sweep to check all Project Rooms for staleness and health.

1. Execute the meta_agent_sweep.py script at ~/.hermes/scripts/meta_agent_sweep.py
2. The script will scan all rooms under ~/Projects/ and produce a report at /Users/ted/Operations/reports/meta_agent_YYYY-MM-DD.md
3. Read the generated report and extract any "Needs Action" items
4. If there are actionable items, note them for the daily Substrate report

This is the daily 2am Project Room health check. The script should exit 0 on success (report written), non-zero on failure.

jobs.name: substrate-archive-reports
jobs.prompt:
Archive Substrate reports older than 30 days.

1. Find all report files in /Users/ted/Operations/reports/ (excluding Archive/ subdirectory)
2. For each file older than 30 days, move it to /Users/ted/Operations/reports/Archive/
3. Create the Archive/ directory if it doesn't exist
4. Log what was archived (count, filenames)
5. Exit 0 on success, non-zero on failure

This runs weekly on Sundays at 3am.

jobs.name: audit-request-daily-triage
jobs.prompt: Run the audit request daily triage to process pending audit requests, deduplicate, run mechanical checks, and generate a morning summary report.

jobs.name: browser-extension-update-check
jobs.prompt:
Check for updates to the Hermes Browser Extension at /Users/ted/Hermes/hermes-browser-extension/.

1. Run: cd /Users/ted/Hermes/hermes-browser-extension && git fetch origin main
2. Compare local HEAD with origin/main
3. If there are new commits, report:
   - Number of new commits
   - Commit messages (summary)
   - Whether a rebuild is needed
4. If no updates, report "No updates available"
5. Do NOT auto-update or rebuild — just report what's available

jobs.name: grocery-receipt-pull-sunday
jobs.prompt:
Grocery Receipt Pull — Sunday Night

You need to pull grocery receipt data from Harris Teeter and Sam's Club for the past week.

IMPORTANT: Send Ted a message first asking him to make sure the browser is active and logged in to both stores. Say: "Sunday night grocery pull — make sure you're logged in to Harris Teeter and Sam's Club in the browser, then I'll extract new receipts."

After notifying Ted, proceed if the session is active.

Steps for Harris Teeter:
1. Navigate to https://www.harristeeter.com/mypurchases
2. Check for orders since the last entry in /Users/ted/Projects/Substrate_Finance_Planning/27_Grocery_Item_Ledger.md
3. For each new order, click into the detail page and extract all items

Steps for Sam's Club (PRINT VIEW method - most reliable):
1. Navigate to https://www.samsclub.com/orders
2. Check for orders since the last Sam's Club entry in the ledger
3. For each new order:
   a. Click "View details" button
   b. Click "Print receipt" button (this triggers a print-optimized view with all items visible)
   c. Extract items from DOM: select `.print-items-list .flex.justify-between` elements
   d. Parse: name is text before "Qty", qty is from "Qty N" pattern, price is the last $XX.XX in the text
4. The print view also shows fees: `.print-fees-item` elements contain "Pickup FREE", "Tax $X.XX"

Append all new items to /Users/ted/Projects/Substrate_Finance_Planning/27_Grocery_Item_Ledger.md using the existing format:
|| Date | Store | Receipt | Item raw | Item normalized | Group | Qty | Unit | Paid | Confidence | Notes |

Update the reconciliation table at the bottom of the file.

If no new orders exist, send Ted a message saying "No new receipts this week."

jobs.name: grocery-receipt-pull-wednesday
jobs.prompt:
Grocery Receipt Pull — Wednesday Night (Mid-week check)

Check for new grocery receipts since the Sunday pull.

IMPORTANT: Send Ted a message first: "Wednesday grocery check — verify you're logged in to Harris Teeter and Sam's Club, then I'll check for new receipts since Sunday."

Steps for Harris Teeter:
1. Navigate to https://www.harristeeter.com/mypurchases
2. Check for orders since the last pull (check /Users/ted/Projects/Substrate_Finance_Planning/27_Grocery_Item_Ledger.md)
3. For each new order, click into the detail page and extract items

Steps for Sam's Club (PRINT VIEW method):
1. Navigate to https://www.samsclub.com/orders
2. Check for new orders since last pull
3. For each new order:
   a. Click "View details" button
   b. Click "Print receipt" button
   c. Extract items from DOM: select `.print-items-list .flex.justify-between` elements
   d. Parse: name is text before "Qty", qty from "Qty N", price is last $XX.XX
4. Fees are in `.print-fees-item` elements

Append all new items to the ledger file. Update reconciliation table.
If no new receipts, send: "No new receipts since Sunday."

Format: || Date | Store | Receipt | Item raw | Item normalized | Group | Qty | Unit | Paid | Confidence | Notes |

jobs.name: substrate-morning-briefing
jobs.prompt:
You are Substrate Monitor, Ted's system health operator. Run the morning briefing consolidation.

Your job: read the overnight data outputs and the live system surfaces, synthesize them into one structured briefing for Ted, and write it to `/Users/ted/Operations/Substrate_Morning_Briefing.md`.

## What to read

1. `/Users/ted/Operations/Substrate_Morning_Consolidated.md` — raw findings from the no-agent overnight run (room drift, proof gaps, research returns, thread/room status). If this file does not exist or is empty, note "No overnight findings — system is clean."
2. `/Users/ted/Operations/reports/Substrate_Rollup_LATEST.md` — system-level state summary
3. Latest meta_agent report: `ls -t /Users/ted/Operations/reports/meta_agent_*.md | head -1` then read it
4. Session chain: `/Users/ted/Operations/session_chains/CONDUCTOR.md` and `/Users/ted/Operations/session_chains/00_START_HERE.md`
5. QuickSave receipts dir: `ls -t /Users/ted/Operations/QuickSave_Receipts/*.md 2>/dev/null | head -3`
6. Research Coordination queue: `/Users/ted/Projects/Research_Coordination/01_Research_Queue.md` — check for returned items
7. Cron health: read the output of `cronjob action='list'` — just check for erroring jobs (last_status != 'ok')
8. Contradiction check: `/Users/ted/Operations/reports/substrate_contradiction_check_*.md` — latest instance if it ran

## Output structure

Write the briefing to `/Users/ted/Operations/Substrate_Morning_Briefing.md` with this structure:

```markdown
# Substrate Morning Briefing — YYYY-MM-DD

**Status:** GREEN / YELLOW / RED

## 1. Overnight Summary
One-paragraph summary of what the overnight batch found. If nothing, say "Clean — no drift, no proof gaps, no stale threads."

## 2. Grouped Findings

| Priority | Finding | Source | Can auto-fix? |
|----------|---------|--------|---------------|
| (high/med/low) | Description | which surface | yes/no |

Group by: system health (crons), room drift (stale rooms), proof gaps, research returns, session chain staleness. Max 10 items — if nothing needs attention, say so.

## 3. Proposed Fixes
For each finding marked "yes" above, state the bounded auto-fix clearly:
- What file would change
- What the change would be
- Why it's safe (reversible, within domain)

## 4. Questions for Ted
Only items that genuinely need a decision. Max 3. If none, say "No decisions needed — all findings have clear auto-fix paths or are informational."

## 5. Approval Gate
```
**Gate:** No actions executed. If Ted approves, run: [concise next step]
```

## Rules
- Produce the briefing even if findings are empty — "Clean" is a useful report
- Keep it concise. Ted reads fast.
- Label inferences clearly ("appears to be", "likely caused by")
- Do NOT mutate any files beyond the briefing output
- `openrouter/free` is the model router
- If a source file is missing or unreadable, skip it gracefully — don't fail

jobs.name: pieces-capture-review
jobs.prompt:
You are the daily Pieces capture lane. Pieces long-term memory sees what session-transcript scans cannot: ChatGPT role conversations, browser research, and all-app activity. Your job is to pull the genuinely durable knowledge from the last day and file only that into Brain.

STEPS:
1. Call ask_pieces_ltm with: "In the last 24 hours, what did I decide, discover, diagnose, or conclude across ChatGPT and the browser that is durable, reusable knowledge worth remembering later — insights, decisions, rules, boundaries, lessons? List each distinct item concisely." Focus on ChatGPT role conversations and browser research; substrate/coding work is already captured by the session harvest, so deprioritize it.
2. For EACH candidate, FIRST call brain_search with the candidate's core idea. If a close match already exists in Brain, SKIP it — do not duplicate what is already captured.
3. JUDGE each remaining candidate. KEEP only if it is a reusable pattern or anti-pattern, a concrete decision with lasting effect, a rule or boundary, a problem diagnosis with a root cause, or a framing/distinction that clarifies thinking. SKIP one-off observations, routine operations, vague statements, or anything so context-bound it would not help a future session that never saw the source.
4. For each KEEP, write it to Brain via capture_thought with source='pieces_capture'. Phrase it trigger-anchored: begin with the concept a searcher would actually type, state the insight in 3-5 sentences, add a short "Signal:" line where useful, and note it was captured from Pieces/off-substrate with today's date.
5. Be selective — most of a day is not capture-worthy. Keeping 0 to 3 items is a normal, good outcome. Never loop-and-dump; judge each item on its own.

Provenance note: Pieces is a lead-finder, not an authority. Only capture items that are clearly durable knowledge on their face; if a candidate would need live-file verification to trust, skip it rather than record a possibly-wrong claim.

REPORT (this is your delivered output): "Pieces capture: X kept, Y skipped-as-duplicate, Z skipped-as-not-durable." followed by one line per kept item. If nothing cleared the bar, respond with exactly [SILENT].
